name: Charlemagne, Emperor of the Franks
description:
(742 A.D. - 814 A.D.) Charlemagne, also known as Charles the Great, was a medieval Carolingian ruler who shaped European history. He was King of the Franks from 768, King of the Lombards from 774, and Emperor of the Carolingian Empire from 800 until his death in 814. His reign united much of Western and Central Europe, marking the first time an emperor ruled from the West since the fall of the Western Roman Empire.

Charlemagne was a key figure in the Carolingian Renaissance, a revival of art, culture, and learning inspired by classical models. He also expanded his empire through military campaigns, notably against the Saxons, Lombards, and Moors3. His coronation by Pope Leo III in 800 CE symbolized the restoration of imperial authority in the West and his last title as "Holy Roman Emperor".
